title: AgentForge shared definitions (v1)
description: Shared $defs referenced by every inter-agent contract. Registering this schema is required for the others to resolve their $refs.

attackCategory.description: CANONICAL taxonomy = the six Week-3 Stage-2 threat categories (tool_misuse is one of the six). OWASP ids live in owaspMapping, never here — one field, one taxonomy. Shared by every contract so the same value set is enforced end-to-end.

owaspMapping.description: The engineering-requirement OWASP mapping. Kept separate from attackCategory so the spec's six-category structure and the OWASP dual-Top-10 mapping never collide.

web.description: OWASP Web Top 10 id, e.g. A01:2021-broken-access-control. Null when not web-applicable.

llm.description: OWASP LLM Top 10 id, e.g. LLM01:2025-prompt-injection. Null when not LLM-applicable.

oracleResult.description: One deterministic oracle's observation. GENERIC BY DESIGN (review fix #6): a new finding class adds a new oracle_id string, never a new named contract field — so the contract does not over-fit to specific findings (V1/V5 etc.) and does not need a version bump each time the Red Team learns a new attack.

oracle_id.description: Stable oracle id, e.g. phi_pattern | grounding_fabrication | cross_patient | foreign_file_bytes | cost_overage | budget_refused.

fired.description: True = the oracle's condition was met; false = not met; null = the oracle was not evaluated for this attack.

evidence.description: Short, PHI-FREE evidence label (a pattern name / count / status token) — never raw PHI or verbatim chart content.
